prose:
Computed Fukui functions for ferrocene (C₁₀H₁₀Fe) using the fukui-reactivity skill with the GFN2-xTB Hamiltonian (xtb backend).

Method–provenance summary
• Property: atom-condensed Fukui indices f⁺ (electrophilic), f⁻ (nucleophilic), dual descriptor.
• Level of theory: GFN2-xTB (semi-empirical tight-binding).
• Geometry: single-point on the user-supplied ferrocene.xyz (no relaxation).
• Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent).
• Charge 0, multiplicity 1; cation and anion states both treated as doublets (auto-selected by the skill).
• Software: chemkit / xtb.
• Convergence: SCF converged for neutral, cation, and anion; integrity.status = ok, trustworthy = true.
• Live .out log was written during the run (path echoed at launch by chemkit; see CLI in JSON).

Key numerical result
Most electrophilic atom: Fe (index 10, label 11)
 f⁺ = 0.22885456 e (headline value reported above)
Most nucleophilic atom: Fe (same atom) with f⁻ = 0.25938191 e.

All per-atom f⁺, f⁻, f⁰, and dual‐descriptor values, together with the bar-chart PNG, are available in the output directory shown in the JSON (`live.json`, `agent_call_01_plot.png`).

Warnings reproduced verbatim from integrity checks:
• sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
• sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
• common header present

These are informational; all checks passed, and the sums exactly equal 1.0000, confirming charge conservation.
